project_type: Custom AI system (local LLM + knowledge graph + reasoning engine), multi-language C++17/Python/Shell
build_system: make with single compilation unit. system/mk_entry.cpp #includes all .cpp files. clang++ -std=c++17 -O2 -Wall -Wextra -DDARWIN system/mk_entry.cpp -lcurl -lsqlite3
test_framework: Custom test framework in tests/test_main.cpp with TEST_ASSERT macros. Tests compile as separate binary.
verification_instructions: 1. Run make in MK_OS/ - must compile with zero errors. 2. Run make test - all assertions must pass. 3. Python scripts must run with python3 without import errors.
setup_instructions: Requires clang++ (C++17), libcurl, libsqlite3, python3. All available on this sandbox.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - can install packages. No Docker daemon available for testing. Mac/Linux compilation. Single compilation unit pattern means all new .cpp files that should be part of main binary need to be #included in mk_entry.cpp.
contribution_requirements: Include guards (#ifndef) on every .cpp file. No standalone main() functions in modules included by mk_entry.cpp. Follow existing naming: MK prefix for classes. Knowledge files use pipe-delimited format: source|relation|target|weight
key_patterns: 1. All major classes are in separate .cpp files with #ifndef guards. 2. mk_entry.cpp #includes them all and has the single main(). 3. Knowledge files are .mk format (source|relation|target|weight). 4. Plugins pattern: class with init/process methods, registered in MKSystem struct. 5. Python scripts live in tools/ directory. 6. Shell scripts for system integration in bootloader/ and MK_OS root.
directory_structure: MK_OS/ is the main project. Key dirs: ai_core/ (reasoning, NLP, generation engines), ai_core/hre/knowledge_files/ (.mk knowledge), mk_brain/ (learning, memory, personality, embeddings), network/ (HTTP, search, autonomous agent), plugins/ (telegram, pc_helper, mesh), system/ (entry, daemon, shell, services), Kernel/ (simulated kernel with drivers), security/, llm/, tools/ (utilities), bootloader/ (VM image)
